newsSinceJun6: No company-specific material news found since June 6, 2026 (no earnings, contracts, M&A or regulatory items). Today's +5.35% move to CNY 617.1 looks flow/sector-driven: China semi-equipment names have been rallying since mid-May on the memory price upcycle, the 50% domestic-sourcing mandate for new fab capacity, and NAURA's record-high run (limit-up May 15, market cap above RMB 450B, backlog reportedly booked through Q1 2027). Bernstein Outperform PT CNY 680 and 21 strong-buy ratings underline how consensus the name now is.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance quote and analyst ratings, 002371.SZ
TrendForce: China domestic chip-equipment adoption beats target, led by NAURA/AMEC
Sina Finance: NAURA limit-up record high, market cap above RMB 450B (May 15, 2026)
DigiTimes: NAURA revenue climbs on China chip tool demand, margins tighten (Apr 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a crowding-sensitive alpha list, a +5.35% pop with no new fundamental evidence on the single most consensus China-WFE name (21 strong buys, near 52-week high, +30% YTD) further erodes residual alpha; yesterday's hold predates today's flow-driven rerating.
verdicts.bull: Localization mandate (50% domestic tools for new capacity), backlog booked through Q1 2027, and SMIC 28nm furnace dominance give NAURA the most durable revenue visibility in China WFE; memory upcycle adds a cyclical kicker.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discovered: $63B cap, strong-buy consensus, and price already near Bernstein's CNY 680 target mean the localization story is priced; CPO/foundry-capex linkage remains indirect.
verdicts.bear: Flow-driven momentum unwinds fast in A-share semis; margin compression from rising costs and domestic competition (AMEC, Piotech) plus any export-control or subsidy-policy shift could compress the multiple from record highs.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results/order disclosures confirming 80%-type order growth and continued share gains under the domestic-sourcing mandate.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded-momentum reversal: valuation near analyst targets with flow-driven gains; policy/export-control surprises or memory-capex digestion would hit the multiple hardest.
